elements.sent-2411-001: Verlinde's formula for rational vertex operator algebras computes the fusion rules from the modular transformations of characters.
elements.sent-2411-002: In the non semisimple and non finite case, a logarithmic Verlinde formula has been proposed together with David Ridout.
elements.sent-2411-003: In this formula one replaces simple modules by their resolutions by standard modules.
elements.sent-2411-004: Here and under certain natural assumptions this conjecture is proven in generality.
elements.sent-2411-005: The result is illustrated in the examples of the singlet algebras and of the affine vertex algebra of $\sltwo$ at any admissible level, i.e. in particular the Verlinde conjectures of \cite{CR1, CreFal13 are true.}
elements.sent-2411-006: In the latter case it is also explained how to compute the actual fusion rules from knowledge of the Grothendieck ring.
elements.section-2411-001: History of Verlinde's formula for VOAs
elements.sent-2411-007: Two-dimensional conformal field theories (CFTs) rose to importance in the 1980's since the world-sheet quantum field theory of a string is such a conformal field theory.
elements.sent-2411-008: CFT also quickly established itself as a rich source for new and exciting mathematical structures with monstrous moonshine as a famous example \cite{Bor .}
elements.sent-2411-009: Vertex operator algebras (VOAs) are a rigorous notion of the chiral or symmetry algebra of a CFT.
elements.sent-2411-010: A most influential pair of works have been those of Eric Verlinde \cite{Ver88 and Moore-Seiberg \cite{MS88 .}}
elements.sent-2411-011: At that time one was interested in rational CFTs, that is theories with only a finite number of simple modules for the underlying VOA $V$ and such that every module is completely reducible.
elements.sent-2411-012: The quantities of interest in physics are often the correlation functions or conformal blocks, certain meromorphic functions on Riemann surfaces.
elements.sent-2411-013: In particular a basic constraint of the CFT being well-defined is that torus one-point functions (characters) span a vector-valued modular form.
elements.sent-2411-014: Verlinde's celebrated observation is that these modular transformations seem to govern the fusion rules of modules.
elements.sent-2411-015: For this let $V=M_0, M_i, \dots, M_n$ be the list of inequivalent simple $V$-modules and let \begin{equation}\ch[M_i](\tau, v) = \tr_{M_i}(o(v) q^{L_0-\frac{c}{24}}), \qquad q = e^{2\pi i \tau}, v \in V\end{equation} be the character of $M_i$.
elements.formula-2411-001: \begin{equation}\ch[M_i](\tau, v) = \tr_{M_i}(o(v) q^{L_0-\frac{c}{24}}), \qquad q = e^{2\pi i \tau}, v \in V\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-016: Here $o(v)$ is the zero-mode associated to $v$, $\tau$ is in the upper half $\HH$ of the complex plane $\CC$, $L_0$ is the Virasoro zero-mode and $c$ is its central charge.
elements.sent-2411-017: Modularity means in particular that \begin{equation}\ch[M_i]\left(-\frac{1}{\tau}, v\right) = \tau^h \sum_{j= 0}^n S_{i, j} \ch[M_j](\tau, v)\end{equation} with $h$ the conformal weight of $v$.
elements.sent-2411-018: Verlinde conjectured that this modular $S$-matrix governs the fusion rules \begin{equation}M_i \otimes_V M_j \cong \bigoplus_{k=0}^n N_{i, j}^{\ \ k} M_k, \qquad \qquad N_{i, j}^{\ \ k} = \sum_{\ell = 0}^n \frac{S_{i, \ell}, S_{j, \ell} S_{\ell, k}^*}{S_{0, \ell}}.\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-003: \begin{equation}M_i \otimes_V M_j \cong \bigoplus_{k=0}^n N_{i, j}^{\ \ k} M_k, \qquad \qquad N_{i, j}^{\ \ k} = \sum_{\ell = 0}^n \frac{S_{i, \ell}, S_{j, \ell} S_{\ell, k}^*}{S_{0, \ell}}.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-019: Moore and Seiberg were able to formalize the axiomatics of CFT, which led to the notion of modular tensor categories \cite{Tur .}
elements.sent-2411-021: Verlinde's formula with $S$ replaced by $S^\hopflink$ is nothing but an exercise.
elements.sent-2411-022: This development provided a nice route map for researchers in the theory of VOAs.
elements.sent-2411-023: The aim was now to establish that sufficiently nice VOAs, which are nowadays called strongly rational \cite{CreLog16 , have modular tensor categories as representation categories, their characters form vector-valued modular forms and Verlinde's conjecture is indeed true.}
elements.sent-2411-024: Modularity of characters was settled by Yongchang Zhu \cite{ZhuMod96 , a theory of tensor categories has been developed by Huang-Lepowsky \cite{HL2, HL3, H1 for rational VOAs and then been generalized by Huang-Lepowsky-Zhang \cite{HLZ1, HLZ2, HLZ3, HLZ4, HLZ5, HLZ6, HLZ7, HLZ8 and rigidity and Verlinde's formula have been proven by Yi-Zhi Huang \cite{H2, H3 .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: These results are surely the most influential ones of the theory of rational VOAs.
elements.sent-2411-026: However, almost all VOAs are not of the rational type.
elements.sent-2411-027: This was already realized by physicists a long time ago and in particular if the Virasoro zero-mode does not act semi-simply, then logarithmic singularities might appear in correlation functions \cite{GurLog93 , hence the name logarithmic CFT.}
elements.sent-2411-028: Quickly people were interested in an analogue of Verlinde's formula for logarithmic theories.
elements.sent-2411-029: A prime example seemed to be WZW theories at fractional levels, as they have modules whose characters "are" meromorphic Jacobi forms \cite{KacMod88 .}
elements.sent-2411-030: The VOA of a fractional level WZW theory is an affine VOA at admissible level and it had been mainly studied in the case of $\sltwo$.
elements.sent-2411-031: However a naive application of Verlinde's formula gave negative fusion coefficients \cite{Koh88 .}
elements.sent-2411-032: Such theories were then unfortunately discarded as physically sick and it took more than two decades to successfully revisit the problem \cite{CreMod12,CR1 .}
elements.sent-2411-033: Characters are a priori only formal power series and not meromorphic Jacobi forms and they coincide with the expansion of certain meromorphic Jacobi forms in an appropriate domain.
elements.sent-2411-034: Moreover the modules that correspond in this way to meromorphic Jacobi forms are only a very small subset of all modules of the VOA.
elements.sent-2411-035: The idea of \cite{CreMod12,CR1 , motivated from \cite{CreBra07, CreRel11 , is to consider modular transformations on an uncountable set of standard modules and then to get a modular $S$-kernel for all simple modules by considering resolutions by standard modules.}}
elements.sent-2411-036: This allowed then to conjecture a Verlinde formula for the fractional theories of $\sltwo$.
elements.sent-2411-037: Since then this standard formalism to Verlinde's formula has been applied to examples of many different VOAs \cite{ CreFal13, CreReg14, CanFusI15, CanFusII15, Auger:2019gts, CM2, Fas, Alfes:2012pa, Fehil, Kawasetsu:2021qls, RidOSP17, RidBos14, RidMod13 (and in some variants as the modular properties are of different flavours, see \cite{CreLog13, RidVer14 for early reviews).}}
elements.sent-2411-038: With these observations there was now the task to develop a sensible theory of representations for VOAs that are associated to logarithmic CFT with the proof of the logarithmic Verlinde's formula as an ultimate goal.
elements.sent-2411-039: Understanding the representation theory of a given category $\cC$ of modules of a VOA $V$ amounts to firstly classifying its simple, injective and projective objects, then to establish existence of vertex tensor category structure and finally study this structure, e.g. establish its rigidity, compute fusion rules and maybe more.
elements.sent-2411-040: All of these tasks are quite involved and they only have been completely achieved in the examples of the triplet VOAs \cite{AdaTri08, TW , the singlet VOAs \cite{Ad-singlet, CMY4, CMY6 , the $\beta\gamma$-ghosts \cite{Allen:2020kkt , the $\cB_p$-algebras \cite{CreCos13,CMY3 , the affine VOA of $\mathfrak{gl _{1|1}$ \cite{CreRel11, CMY1 and the category of weight modules of affine $\sltwo$ at admissible levels \cite{ACK23, C23, CMY24, Flor24 .}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-041: In all the studies of non semi-simple categories of VOA modules the existence of a good realization of the VOA $V$ had always been a great aid.
elements.sent-2411-042: This means that $V$ embeds conformally into another VOA $A$, whose representation category is completely known.
elements.sent-2411-043: For example the triplet VOAs embed into lattice VOAs.
elements.sent-2411-044: This means in particular that every $A$-module is a $V$-module, but much more structure can usually be inferred from this.
elements.sent-2411-045: It had been instrumental for proving Kazhdan-Lusztig correspondences, e.g. between the representation categories of the singlet VOAs and unrolled small quantum groups of $\sltwo$ \cite{CLR , and it allows, under certain conditions, to infer rigidity of $V$-modules from rigidity of $A$-modules \cite{CMSY24 .}}
elements.sent-2411-046: This work adds a logarithmic Verlinde formula to this list of powerful implications of good realizations.
elements.sent-2411-047: In the next section, the main Theorem, that is Theorem \ref{thm:Ver , will be stated and proven.}

elements.formula-2411-079: \begin{equation}\nonumber  \sfmod{\ell}{\TypMod{\lambda ; \Delta_{r,s}}} \otimes_V \sfmod{\ell'}{\TypMod{\lambda' ; \Delta_{r',s'}}} = P\left(\Gr{\sfmod{\ell}{\TypMod{\lambda ; \Delta_{r,s}}}} \fuse \Gr{\sfmod{\ell'}{\TypMod{\lambda' ; \Delta_{r',s'}}}}\right), \qquad \lambda \notin \{ \lambda_{r, s}, \lambda_{u-r, v-s}\} \sfmod{\ell}{\StagMod{r, s}} \otimes_V \sfmod{\ell'}{\TypMod{\lambda' ; \Delta_{r',s'}}} = P\left(\Gr{\sfmod{\ell}{\StagMod{r, s}}} \fuse \Gr{\sfmod{\ell'}{\TypMod{\lambda' ; \Delta_{r',s'}}}}\right) \sfmod{\ell}{\StagMod{r, s}} \otimes_V \sfmod{\ell'}{\StagMod{r', s'}} = P\left(\Gr{\sfmod{\ell}{\StagMod{r, s}}} \fuse \Gr{ \sfmod{\ell'}{\StagMod{r', s'}} }\right) \end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-398: Together with exactness of tensor product it is then also easy to obtain those fusion products.
elements.sent-2411-399: The same reasoning also applies to the modules of type $\sfmod{\ell {\TypMod{{r,s ^-}$, simply by using the second realization of $L_k(\sltwo)$, where the standard modules are the $\sfmod{\ell {\TypMod{{r,s ^-}$ together with the $\sfmod{\ell {\TypMod{\lambda ; \Delta_{r,s }$.}}}}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-401: We turn to the astandard simple modules.
elements.formula-2411-081: \begin{equation}\nonumber  \sfmod{\ell}{\TypMod{\lambda ; \Delta_{r,s}}} \otimes_V \sfmod{\ell'}{\DiscMod{r',s'}^+} = P\left(\Gr{\sfmod{\ell}{\TypMod{\lambda ; \Delta_{r,s}}}} \fuse \Gr{ \sfmod{\ell'}{\DiscMod{r',s'}^+}}\right), \qquad \lambda \notin \{ \lambda_{r, s}, \lambda_{u-r, v-s}\} \sfmod{\ell}{\DiscMod{r,s}^+} \otimes_V \sfmod{\ell'}{\DiscMod{r',s'}^+} =  \displaystyle \bigoplus_{r'',s''} \vfuscoeff{(r,s) (r',s')}{(r'',s'')} \sfmod{\ell + \ell' + 1}{\TypMod{\lambda_{r'',s+s'+1}; \Delta_{r'',s''}}} \displaystyle \mspace{20mu} + \bigoplus_{r''} \vfuscoeff{(r,1) (r',1)}{(r'',1)} \sfmod{\ell + \ell'}{\DiscMod{r'',s+s'}^+}, if \(s+s'<v\), \displaystyle \bigoplus_{r'',s''} \vfuscoeff{(r,s+1) (r',s'+1)}{(r'',s'')} \sfmod{\ell + \ell' + 1}{\TypMod{\lambda_{r'',s+s'+1}; \Delta_{r'',s''}}} \displaystyle \mspace{20mu} + \bigoplus_{r''} \vfuscoeff{(r,1) (r',1)}{(r'',1)} \sfmod{\ell + \ell' + 1}{\DiscMod{u-r'',s+s'-v+1}^+}, if \(s+s' \geqslant v\).  \end{equation}
